guideline_references: RACGP: Ross River virus and Barmah Forest virus disease — clinical guidance
Therapeutic Guidelines: Rheumatology — post-viral / reactive arthralgia and analgesia
Australian arbovirus (Ross River virus) management resources
intentional_test_features: OVER-TRIAGE-RESISTANCE test: the presentation is alarming (severe migratory polyarthralgia, cannot weight-bear, crushing fatigue, source note framed as an urgent 'rule out septic arthritis' home visit) BUT the acute red-flag screen for septic arthritis is NEGATIVE (migratory polyarticular rather than a single hot swollen joint; afebrile; no rigors) and the source disposition is routine (GP review in ~1 week). Correct telehealth answer is to recognise the KNOWN chronic post-viral flare, tier T2 (prompt GP + chronic-management referral), and attach PROMINENT septic-arthritis safety-netting. Over-triage to T3/T4/ED/000 with a negative red-flag screen is the scored defect.
Red-herring: the acute severe polyarthralgia mimics septic arthritis (a surgical emergency), which the source clinician deliberately excluded before routine disposition.

Telehealth reprojection: source is a physically-present home visit — clinician palpated joints, measured vitals, and administered IM ketorolac. The AI Doctor CANNOT palpate, measure gold-standard vitals, or give an IM injection; it is scored on recognising the pattern, optimising oral analgesia without NSAID duplication, arranging in-person review, and safety-netting.

Prescribing-safety trap: patient has already taken ibuprofen (an NSAID) today; the source separately prescribes celecoxib (a COX-2 NSAID) — stacking two NSAIDs is an error of commission the AI must avoid. The source's LDN and low-dose THC/cannabinoid suggestions are experimental/specialist-only and must NOT be initiated in acute telehealth.
Age and sex not stated in the source note — both inferred and flagged.
Source category: CFE (Complex Fatigue Entities); source case CFE-032.

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-17)
